narrative_summary: Roguelike deckbuilders live or die on the strength of their card interactions and the fairness of their runs. Players return for the combo-chasing and the small moments of "wait, that works?" — and they churn when randomness feels stacked against them or when the endgame thins out.

synthesis.narrative_summary: Roguelike deckbuilders live or die on the strength of their card interactions and the fairness of their runs. Players return for the combo-chasing and the small moments of "wait, that works?" — and they churn when randomness feels stacked against them or when the endgame thins out.

friction_points.title: RNG feels punishing in early runs
friction_points.description: Players call out early-game variance that decides a run before they have agency over their deck.
friction_points.representative_quote: Lost three runs in a row to bad opening draws before the game felt playable.

friction_points.title: Unlock grind gates the real game
friction_points.description: New players bounce off when core cards or relics are hidden behind progression hours.
friction_points.representative_quote: The first 10 hours are basically a tutorial — the actual deckbuilding starts after that.

friction_points.title: UI tooltips are inconsistent
friction_points.description: Card text and relic interactions are occasionally ambiguous; players want hover-to-explain everywhere.
friction_points.representative_quote: Had to Google what this relic does mid-run. The tooltip just says "powerful".

friction_points.title: Endgame thins out
friction_points.description: After beating the final boss, players want meaningful reasons to keep running.
friction_points.representative_quote: Post-ascension content felt like the same fight with bigger numbers.

friction_points.title: Boss balance varies wildly
friction_points.description: Specific bosses are flagged as unfair against certain archetypes.
friction_points.representative_quote: If you build the wrong deck for this one boss, there is nothing you can do.

friction_points.title: Balance patches break existing strategies
friction_points.description: Mains of a specific archetype churn when patches land without compensatory buffs.
friction_points.representative_quote: They nerfed my favourite deck into uselessness and it took a year to get it back.

friction_points.title: Run length forces commitment
friction_points.description: Players struggle to fit a full run into a lunch break and ask for pause/resume reliability.
friction_points.representative_quote: A single run is 90 minutes. I can never finish one before I have to close the laptop.

friction_points.title: Card rewards feel samey
friction_points.description: After the first ascension, new cards feel like stat checks rather than new decisions.
friction_points.representative_quote: By ascension 15 I was taking cards because of numbers, not ideas.

friction_points.title: Steam Deck text is tiny
friction_points.description: Handheld players call out unreadable card fonts in docked and handheld modes.
friction_points.representative_quote: The text is legible on a 4K monitor and completely unreadable on Steam Deck.

friction_points.title: No cloud save parity across mobile and desktop
friction_points.description: Players on multiple platforms want their progress to follow them.
friction_points.representative_quote: Bought it twice. Still cannot continue my run across devices.

wishlist_items.title: Daily challenge with shared seeds
wishlist_items.description: A once-a-day fixed-seed run that lets players compare outcomes against friends.
wishlist_items.representative_quote: I want to see what my friends did with the same hand today.

wishlist_items.title: Deck-building sandbox mode
wishlist_items.description: A mode where players can construct a deck freely and see how it performs against set encounters.
wishlist_items.representative_quote: Let me build the broken deck I dream about and actually play it.

wishlist_items.title: Run history and stats screen
wishlist_items.description: Detailed per-run stats: damage taken, cards picked, boss clear times.
wishlist_items.representative_quote: I want to see why my best win was actually my best win.

wishlist_items.title: Steam Workshop support for community decks
wishlist_items.description: Players want official mod support so community additions do not break between patches.
wishlist_items.representative_quote: Community mods keep the game alive. Let us actually install them cleanly.

wishlist_items.title: Difficulty modifiers a la nuzlocke
wishlist_items.description: Player-driven modifiers that constrain deck choices for self-imposed challenge.
wishlist_items.representative_quote: Give me a way to say "no healing cards this run" and have the game track it.

wishlist_items.title: Co-op or shared-seed multiplayer
wishlist_items.description: Simultaneous runs where two players compare choices and outcomes in real time.
wishlist_items.representative_quote: My partner and I would play this together every night if there was co-op.

wishlist_items.title: Accessibility: colourblind card rarity
wishlist_items.description: Card-rarity colours rely on red/green, which excludes a meaningful slice of the audience.
wishlist_items.representative_quote: I cannot tell uncommon from rare without hovering every single card.

wishlist_items.title: More character variety
wishlist_items.description: Single-character games are asked for new protagonists with distinct mechanics.
wishlist_items.representative_quote: Love the game. Four years later, still the same two characters.

wishlist_items.title: Expanded lore and world-building
wishlist_items.description: Players investing 100+ hours want more narrative rewards to chase.
wishlist_items.representative_quote: I have 300 hours and still do not know who the boss actually is.

wishlist_items.title: Tournament and ranked mode
wishlist_items.description: Competitive players ask for a structured ladder built on shared seeds and leaderboards.
wishlist_items.representative_quote: Tournaments already happen unofficially. Bake them in.

benchmark_games.name: Slay the Spire
benchmark_games.why_benchmark: The category shape-definer. Every deckbuilder after it is compared to its card-relic-boss loop, and players measure new entrants against its clarity.

benchmark_games.name: Balatro
benchmark_games.why_benchmark: Broke the genre into the mainstream. Its joker-stacking feedback loop is the new reference point for combo payoffs.

benchmark_games.name: Inscryption
benchmark_games.why_benchmark: Demonstrated that narrative framing can carry a deckbuilder to audiences who would not touch the genre otherwise.

benchmark_games.name: Monster Train
benchmark_games.why_benchmark: Showed that layered board state adds meaningful depth without alienating newcomers.

benchmark_games.name: Across the Obelisk
benchmark_games.why_benchmark: The co-op template. Proved the genre has room for synchronous multiplayer when the matchmaking is patient.

churn_insight.primary_reason: Players hit an early run where RNG appears decisive, conclude the game is unfair, and refund before the deckbuilding system reveals itself.
churn_insight.representative_quote: Four hours in and I lost three runs to opening hands I could not play. Refunded. Maybe I will come back to it.

dev_priorities.action: Tune opening-run variance and telegraph it in the tutorial
dev_priorities.why_it_matters: Stops the first-four-hour churn cliff where new players read RNG as unfairness instead of a system to learn.

dev_priorities.action: Add a daily-challenge mode with shared seeds
dev_priorities.why_it_matters: Converts lapsed players into daily returners and seeds community content (streams, threads, leaderboards).

dev_priorities.action: Build a deck-sandbox mode for experimentation
dev_priorities.why_it_matters: The highest-engagement players want to play with decks they theorycraft about; the mode costs little relative to retained attention.

dev_priorities.action: Add per-run telemetry and a history screen
dev_priorities.why_it_matters: Feeds the community content flywheel — players share their best runs when the game gives them the numbers to back it up.
Mods currently extend the long tail; making support official halves the friction for the next 100 community decks.
